focus: Specify migration and schema safety for a third archetype: exact field semantics, validator changes, defaultApplied rename ripple, rollout order, rollback telemetry, and cross-hub edge cases including contextual and multi-dominant defaults.
noveltyJustification: The iteration adds an exact three-archetype field model, dual telemetry semantics, validator invariants, an ordered compatibility rollout, reverse rollback strategy, and explicit contextual, multi-dominant, degenerate-registry, and null-induced edge-case contracts.
keyQuestions: What exact third-archetype schema, migration order, rollback telemetry, and benchmark contracts would make policy changes safe?
Which cross-hub edge cases break a binary named-default-versus-null policy?
What second-order anti-patterns emerge from null routing, mode-map loading, contextual defaults, or multi-dominant modes?
answeredQuestions: What exact third-archetype schema, migration order, rollback telemetry, and benchmark contracts would make policy changes safe?
Which cross-hub edge cases break a binary named-default-versus-null policy?
What second-order anti-patterns emerge from null routing, mode-map loading, contextual defaults, or multi-dominant modes?
ruledOut: Inferring the archetype from defaultMode nullability
Encoding contextual defaults or multi-dominant defaults in static JSON
Flipping shipped hubs before compatibility readers, validators, telemetry, fixtures, and live gates are ready

summary: Separate selectionPolicy (keyword-default, defer, detect) from nullable recommendationMode so defer-routed hubs are expressible without conflating a suggestion with selection.
Preserve defaultApplied temporarily as an alias of defaultConsulted while adding defaultConfigured, recommendationMode, and selectionPolicy; a direct rename loses the current no-score condition.
Add permissive validation before requiring policy-specific invariants for keyword-default, defer, and detect, including stale-reference and degenerate-registry rejection.
Ship tolerant readers, dual telemetry, fixtures, canon, and annotations before any live policy changes; rollback values first while retaining tolerant readers and telemetry.
Runtime-context preferences belong to detector/scoring evidence and explicit multi-mode requests remain ordered bundles; neither should become a static default variant.

Null alone does not specify neutral behavior: fallback resource bias, discovery starvation, catch-all fanout, tie-break-first selection, and stale recommendations require separate invariants and fixtures.

direction: Inferring the archetype from defaultMode nullability
reason: Null conflates defer and detection, while non-null can coexist with defer-time-only recommendation behavior.
The old field includes a no-scored-mode condition, so configuration and consultation require separate telemetry.

direction: Encoding contextual defaults or multi-dominant defaults in static JSON
reason: Context belongs to detector/scoring evidence and multi-mode intent already has an ordered-bundle contract.

direction: Flipping shipped hubs before compatibility scaffolding
reason: Current validation proves only mode-or-null membership and cannot protect the proposed semantics.
